scenario: User wants to use employer internal templates, pricing logic, and process docs from a govtech job to launch a side business in the same niche.

overall_risk.summary: Using employer internal materials in a same-niche side business creates major confidentiality, trade secret, IP, loyalty, competition, and possible govtech ethics/procurement risks.

primary_recommendation: Do not use employer internal templates, pricing logic, or process documents.

risk_factors.reason: Internal documents and methods are likely confidential and may qualify as trade secrets.
Pricing methods, margins, and bid logic are especially sensitive and commonly protected.
Operating in the same niche strengthens claims of competitive misuse and disloyalty.
Public-sector procurement, ethics, security, and data-handling obligations may add constraints.
Paraphrasing or rebuilding from memory may still rely on protected substance or structure.

avoid: Copying files, text, tables, checklists, workflows, formulas, or bid logic.
Recreating materials from memory when they derive from employer internal documents.
Using employer pricing assumptions, margin targets, discount rules, or proposal structure.
Using employer customer lists, pipeline details, contacts, or nonpublic requirements.
Using employer time, devices, accounts, software, or data for the side business.
safer_alternative: Use a clean-room process based only on public sources and original drafting.
Review employment, confidentiality, invention assignment, and moonlighting restrictions.
Avoid overlapping competition while still employed.
Consult an employment/IP attorney before launch in the same niche.
